review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the FedRAMP CM-3 (1) control, focusing on configuration change control through auditing, logging, and notification. However, some function names needed refinement for clarity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology. The changes ensure that the function names are descriptive and follow the established naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly specify the type of configuration change being audited or notified, such as 'configuration_change' instead of just 'change'.
title: Configuration Change Control | Automated Documentation, Notification, and Prohibition of Changes
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP CM-3 (1) control focuses on automated documentation, notification, and prohibition of configuration changes. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that configuration changes are audited and logged, unauthorized changes are prohibited through RBAC, and notifications can be managed through admission controllers and API server webhooks. These functions ensure that configuration changes are tracked and unauthorized changes are prevented, aligning with the control's requirements.
